solution: 
```java
class RecentCounter {
    private Queue<Integer> queue;

    public RecentCounter() {
        queue = new LinkedList<>();
    }
    
    public int ping(int t) {
        queue.offer(t);
        while (queue.peek() < t - 3000) {
            queue.poll();
        }
        return queue.size();
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1) amortized
space complexity: O(N) where N is the number of requests within the 3000 ms window
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a queue to store the timestamps of the requests. For each new request, it adds the timestamp to the queue and removes all timestamps that are outside the [t - 3000, t] range. Since the input t is strictly increasing, we can efficiently maintain the window by removing outdated timestamps from the front of the queue. The number of valid requests is simply the size of the queue.
